title: Arena Warm-Up

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: Honeybees communicate the location of food sources through a waggle dance. A bee returning to the hive performs a figure-eight pattern, waggling its body during the straight portion. The angle of the dance relative to the sun indicates direction, while the duration of the waggle indicates distance.

questions.question: What can you infer about how bees would communicate on a cloudy day when the sun is hidden?
options: They may have difficulty communicating direction accurately
They would use a different type of dance
They would stop collecting food entirely
They would fly higher to see the sun
questions.correctOption: They may have difficulty communicating direction accurately

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: The Great Wall of China was not built all at once. Different dynasties constructed separate sections over more than two thousand years, starting around the 7th century BC. Many sections were built by forced laborers and soldiers, and conditions were so harsh that thousands died during construction.

questions.question: What does the long construction period tell us about the Wall's original purpose?
options: Defense was a persistent concern across many different Chinese rulers and eras
The builders kept making mistakes and starting over
One emperor planned the entire wall from the beginning
The wall was built for tourism purposes
questions.correctOption: Defense was a persistent concern across many different Chinese rulers and eras

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood. Two hearts pump blood to the gills, while the third pumps it to the rest of the body. Their blood contains copper-based hemocyanin instead of iron-based hemoglobin, which is more efficient at transporting oxygen in cold, low-oxygen environments.

questions.question: Why might octopuses be well-suited to deep ocean environments?
options: Their copper-based blood is efficient in cold, low-oxygen conditions found in deep water
Because they have three hearts that make them swim faster
Because blue blood is invisible in deep water
Because they can breathe air when oxygen is low
questions.correctOption: Their copper-based blood is efficient in cold, low-oxygen conditions found in deep water

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: Emma set her alarm for 5 AM even though practice didn't start until 7. She laced up her running shoes in the dark kitchen, grabbed a banana, and slipped out the front door before anyone else was awake. The cold morning air hit her face as she started jogging down the empty street.

questions.question: What can you infer about Emma's attitude toward her sport?
questions.sampleAnswer: Emma is very dedicated to her sport because she wakes up two hours before practice to get in extra training.

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: Kai stared at the birthday invitation on his desk. It was from Ryan, the most popular kid in class. Every other student was talking about the party. Kai picked up the invitation, turned it over, then quietly slid it into his backpack without showing anyone.

questions.question: Why might Kai hide the invitation instead of showing it off like the other students?
acceptableAnswers: he is unsure if he wants to go
he may not feel like he belongs
he is uncertain about the friendship
questions.sampleAnswer: Kai might feel unsure about whether he truly belongs with the popular group, so he hides the invitation rather than drawing attention to it.

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: Mrs. Chen placed the graded tests face-down on each desk. When Aiden turned his over, his face went red. He quickly folded the paper and stuffed it in his bag. 'How'd you do?' his friend Leo whispered. 'Fine,' Aiden said, not meeting his eyes.

questions.question: How did Aiden likely do on the test? What clues tell you this?
questions.sampleAnswer: Aiden likely did poorly on the test because he turned red, quickly hid the paper, and lied to his friend about his score.
